dialog: You see, Neura? You stopped before it was too late.
But the monster said I could get 100% accuracy! I wanted to be perfect!
You preferred being good enough on everything rather than perfect on a few things. That's clever.
So learning isn't about memorizing? It's about understanding patterns?
You've learned the most important lesson, Neura. A model that memorizes doesn't truly learn.
It learns to generalize — to recognize patterns, not points. The real world is full of purple dots you've never seen before.
I think I understand now. It's better to be flexible than perfect. Thank you, everyone!
